name: Zep (Graphiti temporal knowledge graph memory)
context_primitives: Zep advertises a temporal knowledge graph ('Graphiti') that ingests conversational and structured business data, tracks validity intervals for facts/edges, and retrieves subgraph context for agents. Emphasis on **time-aware** correctness (what was true when) vs. flat chunk retrieval.
deployment_pattern: Managed Zep service or self-hosted components per docs; define ontology/domain schema for graph extraction quality.
benchmarks_eval_alignment: Company materials and arXiv:2501.13956 discuss benchmarks including LongMemEval and Deep Memory Retrieval comparisons; numeric superiority claims should be validated independently [uncertain].
ecosystem_integrations: Enterprise data connectors; latency targets marketed sub-200ms P95 for retrieval [uncertain].
differentiators: Temporal graph memory vs. pure vector RAG; targets enterprise facts that evolve (policies, org charts, deals).
significance_for_builders: If your domain has **expiring or superseded facts**, graph+temperature may beat embedding-only memory; expect heavier schema/ops burden.
key_timeline_or_milestones: arXiv preprint Jan 2025 (2501.13956) describing temporal KG architecture for agent memory.
